claim_gate.reason: Smith spectra give exact finite source moments when fully enumerated and identify 2-adic dependency types elsewhere. Exact five-set classification closes source-average fixed fifth order. Sampled tuple probes cannot see exponentially rare configurations, and no uniform order>=6 theorem, growing-order bound, or decoder follows.

falsifiers_triggered: Generic source-average fixed fifth-order excess vanishes by exact five-set Smith classification.
Any sampled absence of dependent Smith types is rejected as a rare-event lower bound.
Any complete finite census is rejected as an asymptotic theorem without a uniform counting argument.
Moment excess without an implicit statistic and witness decoder is not algorithmic progress.
Orders above the source matrix rank at small n are marked as finite dimension-capped artifacts.

source_contract.exactness_boundary: Only complete-exact rows are finite identities; sampled rows are rare-event blind
source_contract.joint_probability: For rows (x,-1) with nonzero Smith factors d_i, Pr[all hits]=prod_i gcd(2^n,d_i)/(2^n)^rank
source_contract.representation_count: C=#{x in {0,1}^m : sum_i a_i x_i=t mod 2^n}

limitations: The theorem averages over uniformly random labels and independent target.
It controls fixed fifth order only.
It does not prove concentration for conditioned low fibers.
It supplies no implicit statistic, decoder, or computational lower bound.
source_fifth_moment_certificates.proof: A dependent five-set contains a unique xor-zero four-set. Rank-three quadruples admit every outside point as an integer-rank-four extension. Each Smith-two quadruple has exactly four additional Boolean vertices in its rational affine span; its other U-8 extensions have rank five and Smith valuations (0,0,0,0,1). Substitution gives excess O((3/4)^n)+O(2^-n) at fixed offset.

summary: Computed 11 complete Smith moment censuses and 29 explicitly rare-event-blind type probes across 40 rows. Exact order-four cross-checks passed 2/2 and exact order-five cross-checks passed 1/1. Source-average fixed fifth order is asymptotically obstructed; order>=6 and growing-order asymptotics remain open.
